result.reason: The existing filtering logic for ancestors, via AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter, uses the general NodeMatchesFilter which checks multiple properties (name, hostname, description, tags). To fulfill 'filter by folder name but list all connections inside' more strictly, a specific name-only check for ancestor folders is needed to avoid unintended matches and ensure only relevant folder name matches trigger the display of their contents.
result.approach: Introduce a new method, NodeMatchesFolderNameFilter, which only checks the node's name against the filter text. Modify AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter to use this new method when checking if a parent container matches the filter, thus ensuring that if a folder name matches, all its descendants are shown, based purely on the folder's name.

attempts.raw_output:
I will address the issue by creating a new `NodeMatchesFolderNameFilter` method that specifically checks if a node's name matches the filter text. This new method will be used within `AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter` to ensure that when filtering by folder name, only the folder's name is considered for displaying its descendants. This refinement will prevent unintended matches from other properties like hostname or description when the user intends to filter strictly by folder name for hierarchical display.
